preview_text:
The model favors Egypt to win at 47%, with New Zealand positioned as the underdog at 23%. The gap between them spans 24 percentage points, suggesting Egypt enters as the clearer favorite, though New Zealand retains meaningful chances in the match.

The 30% draw probability indicates the contest is expected to be moderately competitive rather than heavily weighted toward either side securing victory. The spread across outcomes—no single result exceeding 47%—reflects genuine uncertainty in the outcome, with all three possibilities materially likely rather than one dominating.

Model and market diverge meaningfully. The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both shift probability toward Egypt (58–60% win chance) and away from New Zealand (17–17%), creating gaps of roughly 10 percentage points favoring the Egyptian win case. The model assigns higher odds to a New Zealand victory and a draw. The dashboard does not determine which assessment is correct; the divergence suggests bettors and the statistical model weigh the underlying uncertainty differently.
